2605.11502.title: Robust Biomedical Publication Type and Study Design Classification with Knowledge-Guided Perturbations
2605.11502.paper_summary: Accurately and consistently indexing biomedical literature by publication type and study design is essential for supporting evidence synthesis and knowledge discovery. Prior work on automated publication type and study design indexing has primarily focused on expanding label coverage, enriching feature representations, and improving in-domain accuracy, with evaluation typically conducted on data drawn from the same distribution as training. Although pretrained biomedical language models achieve strong performance under these settings, models optimized for in-domain accuracy may rely on superficial lexical or dataset-specific cues, resulting in reduced robustness under distributional shift. In this study, we introduce an evaluation framework based on controlled semantic perturbations to assess the robustness of a publication type classifier and investigate robustness-oriented training strategies that combine entity masking and domain-adversarial training to mitigate reliance on spurious topical correlations. Our results show that the commonly observed trade-off between robustness and in-domain accuracy can be mitigated when robustness objectives are designed to selectively suppress non-task-defining features while preserving salient methodological signals. We find that these improvements arise from two complementary mechanisms: (1) increased reliance on explicit methodological cues when such cues are present in the input, and (2) reduced reliance on spurious domain-specific topical features. These findings highlight the importance of feature-level robustness analysis for publication type and study design classification and suggest that refining masking and adversarial objectives to more selectively suppress topical information may further improve robustness. Data, code, and models are available at: https://github.com/ScienceNLP-Lab/MultiTagger-v2/tree/main/ICHI
2605.11502.paper_summary_zh: 準確且一致地依據出版類型和研究設計對生物醫學文獻進行索引，對於支持證據綜合和知識發現至關重要。<lb/>先前關於自動化出版類型和研究設計索引的工作主要集中在擴展標籤覆蓋範圍、豐富特徵表示以及提高領域內的準確性，評估通常在與訓練相同分佈的數據上進行。<lb/>儘管預訓練的生物醫學語言模型在這些設置下表現良好，但針對領域內準確性優化的模型可能依賴於表面詞彙或數據集特定的線索，導致在分佈轉移下的穩健性降低。<lb/>在這項研究中，我們引入了一個基於控制語義擾動的評估框架，以評估出版類型分類器的穩健性，並調查結合實體遮蔽和領域對抗訓練的穩健性導向訓練策略，以減少對虛假主題相關性的依賴。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，當穩健性目標被設計為有選擇性地抑制非任務定義特徵，同時保留顯著的方法論信號時，通常觀察到的穩健性與領域內準確性之間的權衡可以得到緩解。<lb/>我們發現這些改進來自兩個互補的機制：（1）在輸入中存在明確的方法論線索時，對這些線索的依賴增加，以及（2）對虛假領域特定主題特徵的依賴減少。<lb/>這些發現突顯了對出版類型和研究設計分類進行特徵級穩健性分析的重要性，並建議精煉遮蔽和對抗目標以更有選擇性地抑制主題信息，可能進一步提高穩健性。<lb/>數據、代碼和模型可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/ScienceNLP-Lab/MultiTagger-v2/tree/main/ICHI
2605.11502.author: Shufan Ming et.al.
2605.11502.authors: Shufan Ming, Joe D. Menke, Neil R. Smalheiser, Halil Kilicoglu
